criteria.label: Write the penalized loss and state L1 vs L2 penalty terms.
criteria.example: Write L_total = L_data + λ Ω(w); L2: Ω = Σ wⱼ²; L1: Ω = Σ |wⱼ|; label shrinkage vs sparsity.

criteria.label: Explain why L2 shrinks weights and L1 can zero them using geometry or intuition.
criteria.example: Sketch or describe: L2 round constraint shrinks all coords; L1 diamond corners hit axes → exact zeros.

criteria.label: Recommend L1 or L2 given an overfitting scenario and modeling goal.
criteria.example: Given 500 genes / 60 patients needing a short biomarker list, recommend L1 sparsity; given collinear economic indicators, recommend L2 shrinkage.

criteria.label: Interpret how changing λ affects bias, variance, and train vs validation error.
criteria.example: Describe: small λ → low bias, overfit risk; large λ → high bias, train and val both rise; tune λ at validation minimum.

criteria.label: Diagnose whether to increase or decrease λ from reported train and validation metrics.
criteria.example: Given train acc 97% and val acc 65%, recommend increasing λ; given train 58% and val 56%, recommend decreasing λ or adding capacity.
